name: Vegetarian Pot Stickers with Dipping Sauce
recipeIngredient: 6 oz Tofu, squeezed to remove moisture
recipeInstructions:
1. For the dipping sauce, combine diced tomato, grated daikon, shiso leaves, chopped green onions, lemon juice, 2 tablespoons of tamari or soy sauce, cumin seed, and chili powder. Chill overnight.
2. In a bowl, mix cooked brown rice, squeezed tofu, finely chopped shiitake mushrooms, basil, black olives, ground red chili pepper, sesame oil, and the remaining 2 tablespoons of tamari or soy sauce. Chill for 30 minutes.
3. Place 1 tablespoon of filling in the center of each pot sticker wrapper. Dampen the edges with water, fold the wrapper in half, and seal the edges securely.
4. Heat a little salad oil in a non-stick skillet over medium heat. Arrange the filled pot stickers in a single layer and cook until the bottoms are browned, about 5-7 minutes.
5. Add 1/4 cup water to the skillet, cover, and reduce the heat to low. Cook on low for 6 minutes, or until the water has evaporated completely.
6. Repeat steps 4 and 5 until all pot stickers have been cooked.
7. Serve with the prepared dipping sauce. Makes about 24 pot stickers.
